title: Identify The Correct Operator Station Push-Button For Left, Right, And Surepost Tipper Actions
summary: Use the documented operator station push-button figure and item mapping to identify which push-button starts each left tipper, right tipper, and Surepost tote cycle, and which push-button is the emergency stop.
when_to_use: Use this reference when an operator needs to confirm which physical operator station push-button corresponds to the left tipper cycle, left Surepost tote cycle, right Surepost tote cycle, right tipper cycle, or the emergency stop before use.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as authorization to operate the tipper beyond identifying the documented push-button functions.
Do not use the emergency stop push-button as a normal operating control.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Visually identify the operator station push-buttons and match each item to its documented function using the figure and table.

safety_notes: The emergency stop button is only used if there is imminent danger to personnel or damage to the equipment.
Do not use the emergency stop push-button as a normal operating control.
Escalate if the physical button arrangement or labeling does not match the documented figure and table.
access_or_tools_needed: Physical or visual access to the operator station push-buttons

access_required: Physical or visual access to the operator station push-buttons

visual_references.description: Primary photo showing the operator station push-buttons and item mapping for left, right, Surepost, and emergency stop functions.
Reference photo showing the operator station location and main operator-facing components.

steps.title: Locate the operator station push-button panel
steps.purpose: Find the correct physical control area before identifying individual push-buttons.
steps.instruction: Go to the operator station and locate the push-button panel shown in the operator station push-button figure.
steps.expected_result: The operator station push-button panel is located and visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The push-button panel at the operator station can be clearly seen for comparison to the documented figure.
steps.failure_condition: The push-button panel cannot be located or cannot be visually matched to the documented operator station area.

actions.label: Locate push-button panel
actions.exact_action: Locate the operator station push-button panel.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The operator station push-button arrangement and item labels.
The operator station area where the push-buttons are part of the operator-facing components.

stop_or_escalate_if: The physical operator station push-button panel cannot be located.
The visible control area does not match the documented operator station figure.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 3-3 shows the operator station push-buttons.
The operator station includes four push-buttons among the operator-facing components.

steps.title: Identify left and right tipper button groups
steps.purpose: Separate the push-buttons into left-tipper and right-tipter groups before identifying individual functions.
steps.instruction: Identify the top two push-buttons as the left tipper controls and the bottom two push-buttons as the right tipper controls.
steps.expected_result: The operator can distinguish which two buttons belong to the left tipper and which two belong to the right tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: Top two buttons are recognized as left tipper controls and bottom two buttons are recognized as right tipper controls.
steps.failure_condition: The button grouping on the physical station does not match the documented top-left/bottom-right assignment.

actions.label: Group buttons by tipper
actions.exact_action: Identify the top two buttons as the left tipper controls and the bottom two buttons as the right tipper controls.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The top two versus bottom two push-button positions in the documented arrangement.

stop_or_escalate_if: The top two and bottom two button groups do not match the documented left/right tipper assignment.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The operator station has four push-buttons and an emergency stop push-button. The top two buttons control the left tipper; the bottom two buttons control the right tipper.

steps.title: Identify item 1 as the left tipper cycle start button
steps.purpose: Confirm the documented function of item 1.
steps.instruction: Using the documented item mapping, identify item 1 as the push-button that starts the left tipper cycle.
steps.expected_result: Item 1 is recognized as the left tipper cycle start button.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can point to item 1 and state that it starts the left tipper cycle.
steps.failure_condition: Item 1 cannot be matched on the physical panel or appears to have a different function than documented.

actions.label: Match item 1
actions.exact_action: Use the documented item mapping to identify item 1 as the left tipper cycle start button.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Item 1 in the push-button photo and its mapping to the left tipper cycle start function.

stop_or_escalate_if: Item 1 cannot be identified on the physical panel.
The physical item 1 location or labeling does not match the documented mapping.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Table 3-2: item 1 starts the left tipper cycle.

steps.healthy_condition: The operator can point to item 2 and state that it starts the left tipper cycle for a Surepost tote.

actions.exact_action: Use the documented item mapping to identify item 2 as the left tipper cycle start button for a Surepost tote.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Table 3-2: item 2 starts the left tipper cycle for a Surepost tote.

steps.purpose: Confirm the documented function of item 4.
steps.instruction: Using the documented item mapping, identify item 4 as the push-button that starts the right tipper cycle.
steps.expected_result: Item 4 is recognized as the right tipper cycle start button.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can point to item 4 and state that it starts the right tipper cycle.
steps.failure_condition: Item 4 cannot be matched on the physical panel or appears to have a different function than documented.

steps.title: Identify item 5 as the emergency stop push-button
steps.purpose: Distinguish the emergency stop from normal tipper cycle controls.
steps.instruction: Identify item 5 as the emergency stop push-button and distinguish it from the normal tipper cycle buttons. Treat it as reserved for imminent danger to personnel or damage to equipment.
steps.expected_result: Item 5 is recognized as the emergency stop push-button and is not confused with normal operating controls.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can point to item 5 and state that it is the emergency stop push-button only for imminent danger or equipment damage risk.
steps.failure_condition: The emergency stop cannot be distinguished from normal cycle buttons, or the physical arrangement does not match the documented figure and table.

actions.label: Match item 5
actions.exact_action: Identify item 5 as the emergency stop push-button and distinguish it from the normal tipper cycle buttons.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Item 5 in the push-button photo and its mapping to the emergency stop push-button.

stop_or_escalate_if: The emergency stop push-button cannot be clearly distinguished from the normal tipper cycle buttons.
The physical button arrangement or labeling does not match the documented figure and table.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Table 3-2: item 5 is the emergency stop push-button.
The emergency stop button is only used if there is imminent danger to personnel or damage to the equipment.

success_criteria: The operator can correctly identify the top two buttons as left tipper controls and the bottom two buttons as right tipper controls.

The operator can correctly identify item 1 as left tipper cycle start, item 2 as left Surepost tote cycle start, item 3 as right Surepost tote cycle start, item 4 as right tipper cycle start, and item 5 as the emergency stop push-button.
The emergency stop push-button is distinguished from normal operating controls.
healthy_conditions: The physical push-button arrangement matches the documented figure and table.
Each documented item number can be matched to the corresponding physical push-button.
The emergency stop is clearly identifiable as a separate safety control.
failure_conditions: The physical button arrangement or labeling does not match the documented figure and table.
A push-button cannot be confidently matched to its documented item number or function.
The emergency stop push-button cannot be clearly distinguished from normal tipper cycle buttons.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the physical button arrangement or labeling does not match the documented figure and table.
Escalate if any push-button function cannot be confidently identified from the source-supported figure and table.
Stop and escalate if there is any uncertainty about the emergency stop push-button identification.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall push-button arrangement, item numbers 1 through 5, and the documented mapping for left, right, Surepost, and emergency stop functions.
Operator station location and surrounding operator-facing components.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The operator station has four push-buttons and an emergency stop push-button. The top two buttons control the left tipper; the bottom two buttons control the right tipper.

Table 3-2: item 1 starts the left tipper cycle, item 2 starts the left tipper cycle for a Surepost tote, item 3 starts the right tipper cycle for a Surepost tote, item 4 starts the right tipper cycle, item 5 is the emergency stop push-button.
